title: Report: Flag football game featuring Tom Brady moved out of Saudi Arabia
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady's flag football game moved from Saudi Arabia to Los Angeles due to Middle East tensions.
The game will be held at BMO Stadium in Los Angeles on March 21, featuring NFL stars like Brady and Jalen Hurts.
The relocation follows US and Israel military strikes on Iran and an Iranian drone attack on the US Embassy in Riyadh.

title: Tell us: What’s been Joe Mazzulla’s best soundbite?
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla is known for delivering memorable soundbites since his tenure in Boston.
Boston.com seeks fan input to determine Mazzulla's best soundbite for an upcoming March Madness-themed sports bracket.
The selection criteria for Mazzulla's best soundbite are subjective, ranging from funniest to most profound.

title: Why this Boston Marathon qualifier is running for the American Heart Association
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Blake Smith, 28, from Church Hill, Tennessee, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the American Heart Association.
Smith, diagnosed with a heart murmur and Idiopathic Hypertrophic Subaortic Stenosis, was inspired to run after a successful stress test at 14.
Participation in the marathon also aims to raise funds for the American Heart Association, which holds personal significance for Smith.

title: Cohasset man charged after allegedly shooting family dog while hunting coyotes from his window
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Christopher Lucas, 59, is charged after allegedly shooting his family's dog while attempting to hunt coyotes in Cohasset.
Lucas fired from a second-floor window without a license and outside the legal hunting season, killing the 14-year-old pet.
His firearms license was suspended, and he faces charges including animal cruelty and hunting violations, with arraignment in Quincy District Court.

title: Greater Boston location of Uno Pizzeria reportedly saved by general manager
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Revere's Uno Pizzeria, closed in January, has reopened after a revamp by general manager Amalia Korom.
Korom purchased, renovated, and reopened the location, bringing back all original staff members.
Revere's location is now the only Uno Pizzeria in Greater Boston, with eight locations remaining in New England.

title: Mass. business confidence turns positive for the first time in a year
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts business confidence rose to 52 in February, marking a positive shift for the first time in a year.
AI productivity gains and corporate profits lifted employer sentiment, despite concerns over inflation and tariffs.
The uptick occurred before recent Middle East conflict escalations, which could impact energy prices and supply chains.

title: NFL analyst is not a fan of the Patriots’ reported free agent deal with Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots reportedly agree to a three-year, $48 million deal with former Jets guard Alijah Vera-Tucker.
The Athletic's Daniel Popper gave the Patriots' signing of Vera-Tucker a 'D' grade due to his injury history.
Vera-Tucker has suffered season-ending injuries in three out of his five NFL seasons, impacting his availability.

title: TD Garden owner sues cannabis dispensary The Boston Garden
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Delaware North, owner of TD Garden, is suing The Boston Garden cannabis dispensary for trademark infringement and brand confusion.
The suit claims the dispensary's name deceives the public, using the famous Boston Garden mark linked to sports venues.
The Boston Garden dispensary, co-founded by actor Jonathan Tucker, argues they filed the name with no initial objection.

title: Former Quincy official pleads guilty to embezzling city funds for 153 pounds of steak tips, studio recordings, framed self-portrait
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Thomas Clasby Jr., former Quincy Elder Services director, pleaded guilty to embezzling city funds for personal expenses over several years.
Clasby misused city funds on items including steak tips, studio recordings, and a self-portrait, totaling tens of thousands of dollars.
Charges include embezzlement and fraud, with potential sentences up to 20 years; sentencing is scheduled for June 17.

title: ‘We do feel like we’re being invaded’: Nantucket agrees to try restricted hours at popular tourist destination
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Nantucket restricts Sconset Bluff Walk hours to 8:30 a.m. to 7:30 p.m. after complaints of tourist intrusion.
The Siasconset Civic Association planning to hire a 'bluff docent' for visitor education during July and August.
Proposals include narrower entryways to discourage bikes, with efforts to balance public and private rights on the path.
